issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see where focus is placed, breaking navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which button has focus, preventing interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: Add a visible focus style, e.g., box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate the button accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 452: Increase min-height to at least 24px, e.g., min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px and min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target. This makes the icon button difficult to tap for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 462: Increase min-width and min-height to at least 24px, e.g., min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px and height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target. Users with motor impairments may miss the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: Increase width and height to at least 24px, e.g., width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This does not hide content visually and may expose it to screen readers incorrectly, breaking semantic navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 285: Replace with a correct visually hidden pattern, e.g., position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition on hover without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from the movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: Extend the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to include .hero-image img { transition: none; } and .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can reduce readability for users with visual or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 80: Remove or set word-spacing to a non-negative value, e.g., word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Relying solely on colour to distinguish links fails users with colour blindness or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: Add a non-colour indicator, e.g., text-decoration: underline; or provide a clear underline on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes focus indicators for keyboard users, making it impossible to determine which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: Add a visible focus indicator, such as box-shadow or border, to the :focus-visible rule.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide the element from the visual viewport. Screen reader users may be confused by duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix: Replace the .sr-only class with a correct implementation, such as: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read, especially for people with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 244: Remove the negative word-spacing or set it to a non-negative value.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class sets background-color: #5588bb and color: #ffffff, which gives a contrast ratio of 3.5:1. This is below the required 4.5:1 for normal text, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: Adjust the background colour to be darker or the text colour to be lighter to achieve a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The rule for a sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. This relies solely on colour to distinguish links, which is insufficient for users with colour blindness or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252: Add an underline to links by default, or provide a non-colour visual differentiator such as a border-bottom or font-weight increase.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search button has a width and height of 28px, which is below the recommended minimum touch target size of 44px. This can make the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 497: Increase the width and height to at least 44px, or ensure that the button has sufficient padding to meet the 44px minimum.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px and padding that results in a total height of 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target. This can make the button difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: Increase the min-height and/or padding so that the total height is at least 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, and with padding, the total dimensions are 40px, which is below the 44px minimum. This can make the button difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: Increase the min-width and min-height to at least 24px and adjust padding so that the total dimensions are at least 44px.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes a transition on transform, which can cause motion. There is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable or reduce this motion, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables the transform transition for .btn.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transition on transform and box-shadow that can cause motion. The existing reduced-motion media query does not disable this transition, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: In the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 555, add a rule for .hero-image img that sets transition: none; or disables the transform and box-shadow transitions.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions in the mobileNav module change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav module's open() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In the close() function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function updates visual active states for billing period buttons but does not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle button groups.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState(), after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: Product filter count updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible product count but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may miss the updated filter results count.
issuesFound.fix: After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); in applyFilters(), add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function marks fields as invalid visually and with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In the else block of _renderResult(), after srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); In the valid block, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for mobile navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(), after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for dropdown menus
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state of dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register(), after setting aria-haspopup, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1763: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after _open = true;
Line 1771: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product count and shows/hides cards but does not announce these changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users may not know that the filter has been applied and the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2249: add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Filter applied: ${s.activeCategory}`); after emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function adds CSS classes for invalid fields but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users will not be alerted that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3518: add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); inside the else block after srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function clears error classes for valid fields but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. Screen reader users may continue to hear an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3515: add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false); after srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.init function sets initial open/closed state on FAQ items but does not ensure that aria-expanded is set on the trigger buttons for closed items. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3129: add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, item.open); inside the forEach loop after setting aria-expanded based on shouldBeOpen.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function changes the active billing period but does not update aria-pressed on the period toggle buttons. Screen reader users will not know which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2557: add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive); inside _syncToggleState after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes the element visible to sighted users and removes it from the accessibility tree. Screen‑reader users lose the hidden label or heading.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non‑colour cue
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule “a { text-decoration: none; }” removes the default underline from all links. No alternative visual cue (border, background, or font‑weight) is provided, so users relying on colour alone may not recognise links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector “:focus-visible” sets “outline: none” without providing any other visible focus styling. Keyboard users receive no indication of focus on interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Button focus style removed
issuesFound.explanation: “.btn:focus-visible” removes the outline and does not replace it with another visible focus style, leaving button focus invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Filter‑tab button focus style removed
issuesFound.explanation: “.filter-tabs button:focus-visible” sets “outline: none” with no alternative visual focus indicator, making the tab buttons indistinguishable when focused.

issuesFound.title: FAQ toggle button focus style removed
issuesFound.explanation: “.faq-item dt button:focus-visible” removes the outline and provides a negative outline‑offset, resulting in no visible focus indication for the accordion buttons.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus style removed
issuesFound.explanation: “.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible” removes the outline without supplying an alternative focus visual, leaving the toggle invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on hero image lacks reduced‑motion guard
issuesFound.explanation: “.hero-image img” animates its transform property (perspective/rotate) with a transition, but no @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion) rule disables or reduces this motion, causing issues for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hover motion on hero image lacks reduced‑motion guard
issuesFound.explanation: “.hero-image img:hover” changes the transform, creating motion without a reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition lacks reduced‑motion guard

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The script that opens and closes dropdown menus (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`) adds/removes CSS classes but never updates the `aria‑expanded` state on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are expanded/collapsed via `open(item)` and `close(item)`, but the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger (`item.trigger`) is never set, breaking the required name‑role‑value pattern for accordions.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search component shows and hides the suggestion list, but the `<input>` never receives an updated `aria‑expanded` value, so assistive technologies cannot tell whether the list is visible.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs are toggled with visual CSS (`CSS.active`) but the required `aria‑pressed` state is never set, preventing screen‑reader users from knowing which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “Monthly/Quarterly/Annual” buttons receive a visual `CSS.active` class, but the `aria‑pressed` attribute is never synchronized, leaving the current period ambiguous to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑period toggle does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The binary “monthly ↔ annual” switch toggles a CSS class on the track element but never updates `aria‑pressed` on the toggle control, breaking the ARIA button pattern.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion listbox options lack aria‑selected updates
issuesFound.explanation: When navigating the suggestion list with arrow keys, the script toggles a visual class but never updates the `aria‑selected` attribute on each `<li role="option">`, so screen‑readers cannot announce the current selection.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list options lack aria‑selected updates
issuesFound.explanation: The same issue as Issue 8 applies to the live search dropdown; the ARIA‑selected state is never reflected, preventing proper announcement of the highlighted suggestion.
issuesFound.fix: (same code as Issue 8 – the `setActiveIndex` function already handles both lists after the fix above).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded false
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is rendered with `data-faq-open` false (default), the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to “false”, violating the required initial state for collapsible sections.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← added
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded false
issuesFound.explanation: New dropdown triggers are given `aria-haspopup` but never receive an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so assistive technologies cannot infer the collapsed state on load.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← added
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button lacks initial aria‑expanded false
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button is not given an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` on page load, leaving its state undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← added
```

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input lacks initial aria‑expanded false
issuesFound.explanation: The search box never receives an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` when the suggestion panel is hidden initially, violating the ARIA pattern for comboboxes.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← added
```


These fixes bring the script into compliance with the required ARIA state management patterns and prevent the identified accessibility regressions.

fpTitles: Simple billing‑period toggle does not update aria‑pressed
Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded false
Mobile‑navigation toggle button lacks initial aria‑expanded false

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the mobile navigation drawer, but they never set the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is currently expanded. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (keyboard operability) and ARIA rule 4 (name + state must be exposed).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not toggle aria‑expanded on their question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `faqAccordion.close(item)` functions animate the answer panel but never set `aria‑expanded` on the associated trigger (`item.trigger`). Without this ARIA state, users of screen readers cannot know which FAQ entry is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox does not set aria‑expanded when the suggestion list is shown
issuesFound.explanation: `siteSearch._open()` marks the internal `_searchState.open = true` and shows the suggestion list, but the search `<input>` never receives `aria‑expanded="true"`. When the list is hidden, the attribute is not reset either. This breaks the ARIA combobox pattern, preventing screen‑reader users from perceiving the open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function adds aria‑haspopup and aria‑controls to the trigger but never updates aria‑expanded when the panel is opened or closed. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the disclosure state.
issuesFound.fix: In register() add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. In _openInstance() set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and in _closeInstance() set it back to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands a FAQ item and animates the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, nor does faqAccordion.close() reset it to `"false"`. This violates ARIA disclosure requirements.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open() add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in faqAccordion.close() add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() marks fields as valid/invalid by adding CSS classes and error messages, but never sets the required `aria-invalid` attribute on invalid inputs. Screen readers cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the “else” branch of _renderResult(), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; in the “if (result.valid)” branch, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` (or set to `'false'`).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes error text but does not clear the `aria-invalid` attribute, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In the “if (result.valid)” branch of _renderResult(), after removing the error message, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` (or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false')`).

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised to false on page load for disclosure widgets
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger elements for dropdown menus, the mobile navigation button, and FAQ items receive `aria-haspopup`/`aria-controls` but are never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value. Without this, screen readers may assume the content is already visible.
issuesFound.fix: In each registration/initialisation block, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` (e.g., after setting `aria-haspopup` in dropdownMenu.register, after creating the mobile nav button in mobileNav.init, and after creating FAQ triggers in faqRegistry.parse).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce updates via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates price numbers, period labels, and savings badges but never announces the change to a screen‑reader. Users of assistive technology may not notice the billing period switch.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) || \`Billing period switched to \${period}\`)` to announce the change.

issuesFound.title: FAQ item open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() animate panels but do not provide an ARIA live announcement of the expanded/collapsed state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? (STRINGS.faqOpened?.replace('{title}', item.question) || \`\${item.question} – answer expanded\`) : (STRINGS.faqClosed?.replace('{title}', item.question) || \`\${item.question} – answer collapsed\`))`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in product filter not announced
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the product count badge and empty‑state message without using an ARIA live region, so changes are invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, set its `aria-live="polite"` and update its text via `liveRegion.announce(\`\${count} products shown\`)`. Similarly, announce when the empty‑state appears.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.fix: Line 828: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` rule sets `min-height: 28px` and `min-width: 28px`. While 28 px is above 24 px, the padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8 px) reduces the interactive area to 12 px, which is below the 24 px minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 870: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44 px recommended touch target size. The padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8 px, `var(--space-3)` = 12 px) results in a 28 px height, which is insufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line 885: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44 px recommended touch target size. The padding (`var(--space-2-5)` = 10 px, `var(--space-4)` = 16 px) results in a 28 px height.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44 px recommended touch target size. The padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8 px, `var(--space-5)` = 20 px) results in a 28 px height.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1550: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 28px`, which is below the 44 px recommended touch target size. The padding (`var(--space-2)` = 8 px, `var(--space-5)` = 20 px) results in a 28 px height.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets `min-height: 60px`, which is above 44 px, but the padding (`var(--space-5)` = 20 px, `var(--space-6)` = 24 px) results in a 60 px height. However, the interactive area is sufficient, but the rule also sets a fixed height, which may not accommodate larger touch targets in all contexts.
issuesFound.fix: (No change needed for size; the height is sufficient. This is a false positive for size, but the rule is included for completeness.)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` and `.hero-image img:hover` rules apply a transform animation (`rotateY`, `rotateX`) and box-shadow transition, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 1170–1180) is empty and does not disable or slow the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1170: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; box-shadow: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card:hover` rule applies a `transform: translateY(-2px)` animation, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 1310–1320) only resets `transform` and does not disable the transition. The transition itself (`transition: transform var(--transition-base)`) is not overridden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card:hover` rule applies a `transform: translateY(-3px)` animation, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 1610–1620) only resets `transform` and does not disable the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1610: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card--featured:hover` rule applies a `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` animation, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 1910–1920) only resets `transform` and does not disable the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1910: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-item:hover` rule applies a `transform: translateY(-2px)` animation, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (lines 1760–1770) only resets `transform` and does not disable the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1760: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item:hover { transform: none !important; transition: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button::after` rule applies a `transform: rotate(90deg)` animation on hover, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block (line 2270) only disables the transition, not the transform itself. The transform is still applied, which may cause motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2270: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transform: rotate(90deg) !important; transition: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links, and no alternative non-colour visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can cause words to overlap or become difficult to read, especially for users with cognitive or visual impairments. This violates WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which can cause characters to overlap or become difficult to read, especially for users with low vision or dyslexia. This violates WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: `body { letter-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px on html
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` rule sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the 16 px (1 rem) minimum recommended for readability. This violates WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible. The correct implementation should use `position: absolute`, `width: 1px`, `height: 1px`, `clip: rect(0 0 0 0)`, and `clip-path: inset(50%)`. The current implementation fails to hide content visually and may expose it to all users.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `navToggle` element (line 12345: `const _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`) is a `<div>` or `<span>` styled as a button but lacks keyboard event handlers for Space/Enter. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` (line 12360) toggles the mobile menu but does not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the menu state.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (line 12345) does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set on page load. Screen readers cannot announce the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` (line 12360) announces "Navigation menu opened" but does not update an ARIA live region. The announcement is not guaranteed to be heard by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12368: Use the shared liveRegion module:
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function (line 14500) returns validation errors but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. Screen readers cannot announce invalid state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function (line 14500) does not clear `aria-invalid` when validation passes. Screen readers continue to announce invalid state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick()` function (line 13520) toggles the billing period but does not update `aria-pressed` on the toggle element. Screen readers cannot announce the state.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element (line 13500) does not have `aria-pressed` set on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13510: Set initial aria-pressed:
```
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));
```

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` functions in the `testimonialsCarousel` module (lines 10213–10230) are attached to a non-native interactive element (the carousel root) using `addEventListener('pointerdown', ...)` and `addEventListener('pointerup', ...)`. These functions handle swipe gestures but do not include matching keyboard handlers (`keydown` or `keyup`) for Enter or Space keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe functionality, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` functions in the `testimonialsCarousel` module (lines 12235–12280) are attached to pointer events on the carousel root element. These functions handle swipe gestures for navigation but do not include matching keyboard handlers (keydown/keyup) for Enter and Space keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe actions, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module (lines 11300–11399) toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the open/closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11315: In `_openInstance`, add:
    ```
    setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
    ```
    Line 11325: In `_closeInstance`, add:
    ```
    setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function in the `pricing` module (lines 10300–10399) updates the billing period label (e.g., "Monthly" → "Annual") but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who rely on assistive technologies cannot perceive the dynamic update, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10350: After updating the UI, add:
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(
      period === 'month'
        ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'
        : STRINGS.pricingAnnual  || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%'
    );
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module (lines 11000–11099) does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the mobile navigation trigger button. Screen readers may announce the button as expanded by default, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11050: In `init`, after discovering `_trigger`, add:
    ```
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but it uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, making content fully visible. This breaks the accessibility pattern for skip links and labels.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses text and significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector removes underlines (`text-decoration: none`), relying solely on color to distinguish links. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 175: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement (Global)
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without defining a replacement border or box-shadow. This removes the focus indicator for all interactive elements, blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without a visible replacement, overriding the global focus style and hiding focus state on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a replacement color, border, or box-shadow. This makes keyboard focus invisible for all interactive elements that do not have a more specific override adding a visible style, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border-color. Combined with the global focus reset, this renders keyboard focus invisible on buttons unless the .focus-ring-brand utility is explicitly added.
issuesFound.fix: Line 493: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation links without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple navigation link selectors (account nav, footer nav, social links) set outline: none on :focus-visible without defining a visible color or shadow. This relies on the broken global focus style, making focus invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which leaves content fully visible on the page instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technology. This clutters the interface and defeats the purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Root font size set below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element font-size is set to 12px, making the base 1rem equal to 12px. This results in body text rendering at 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability, especially for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 209: html { font-size: 16px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs text readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This can cause text to overlap or become difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or when zoomed, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 229: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1273: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition defined, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 477 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 477: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without sufficient non-color indicator
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as users who cannot perceive color differences may not identify these as links.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1291: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { text-decoration: underline; }
  Line 1292: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration-thickness: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors set `outline: none` on `:focus-visible` without providing a visible alternative (border, box-shadow, or outline color). This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, blocking navigation access (WCAG 2.1.1, 2.4.11). The global rule (Line 337) affects all elements not specifically overridden, and component rules (buttons, inputs, links) compound the issue.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`. This fails to visually hide the content, causing screen-reader-only text to appear on screen and clutter the layout (WCAG 1.3.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link` uses `transform: translateY(-200%)` and `transition: transform`. The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at Line 600 is empty and does not disable this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision (WCAG 1.4.8).
issuesFound.fix: Line 157: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: `.section-header p` uses `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` (#aaaaaa) on `background-color: var(--colour-bg-page)` (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is ~2.8:1, which fails WCAG 1.4.3 (requires 4.5:1 for normal text).
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: .section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` sets `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`. This is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Global `a` selector and specific nav/footer links set `text-decoration: none`. Hover states rely on color change alone (e.g., Line 556, 904, 917), which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger element does not update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1713: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1743: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not receive focus on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the `mobileNav.open` function is called, focus is not moved into the `_drawer` element. Keyboard users may remain focused on the trigger button while the menu overlay covers the content, violating focus management requirements for dialogs/drawers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1713: const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not regain focus on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the `mobileNav.close` function is called, focus is not returned to the `_trigger` button. Keyboard users may lose their place in the navigation flow after closing the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1743: if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but does not initialize `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. The initial state is not exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1967: if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger elements do not update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on `inst.trigger`. Users are not notified of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1908: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1922: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on `item.trigger`. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` helper function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to announce validation errors.

issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function updates the text content and `aria-label` of the badge, but since the badge is not a live region, the change is not announced. Users are not informed when the product count changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2063: const liveRegion = qs('[aria-live="polite"]'); if (liveRegion) liveRegion.textContent = `${count} products found`;

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick` function in `scrollToTop` scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action via a live region. Users may be unsure why the viewport changed.

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static and width/height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text becomes visible to all users, breaking the intended accessibility pattern. The class must use absolute positioning and clipping to hide content visually while preserving it for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only-focusable utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable class inherits the broken .sr-only styles (position: static, width/height: auto), causing focusable skip links or screen-reader text to remain visible on the page instead of being visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing an alternative visible focus indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus when navigating with Tab key.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: .btn:focus-visible removes the default outline with outline: none and provides no replacement box-shadow or border, making focused buttons invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link's :focus-visible state sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users navigating to the site logo.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the toggle has focus.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The search form submit button sets outline: none in its :focus-visible state without a replacement visual indicator, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation remove the focus outline without providing an alternative visible focus style, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the focus outline without replacement, making keyboard navigation invisible to users.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons set outline: none in their :focus-visible state without providing a visible alternative focus ring or border.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without replacement, hiding focus state from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it difficult for keyboard users to see when the scrollable region has focus.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none in their :focus-visible state without a visible replacement, hiding focus from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The footer brand links remove the focus outline without providing an alternative visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the focus outline without replacement, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Social links focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative focus style.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required for WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size and potentially causing activation difficulties for users with tremors or limited precision.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size for comfortable touch activation.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu touch targets below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links and buttons have min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Search input touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search input field has an explicit height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended minimum touch target size for input fields.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, falling below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removal without alternative indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none without providing alternative non-color visual differentiators (such as underline on hover/focus or border-bottom) for many link instances, causing links to be distinguished by color alone.

issuesFound.title: Navigation links rely on color alone for identification
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu links remove underlines and only change color on hover (var(--colour-brand-primary)), providing no non-color visual indicator that they are interactive links.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines and rely solely on color change (to white) on hover, failing to provide a non-color visual differentiator for link identification.

issuesFound.title: Social links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines and only change color on hover, providing no visual indicator other than color to distinguish them as interactive links.

issuesFound.title: Button active state motion lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active, creating motion animation that is not disabled or overridden in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block, potentially causing issues for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties and applies perspective transforms on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 737 is empty and fails to disable the motion animation.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on required note text
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-note class uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against the white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum required for WCAG AA compliance.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, below the 4.5:1 required for normal text at 16px.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on outline button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-outline class uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against a transparent background (effectively white #ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum for AA compliance.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on ghost button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-ghost class uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against a transparent background (white), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, well below the 4.5:1 required for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on required marker
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-marker uses color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) which against a white background provides only approximately 2.2:1 contrast ratio, below the 4.5:1 minimum for text.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible and making it impossible to track focus position.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without replacement. High contrast mode users rely on visible focus indicators, and this removes them entirely, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (Enhanced).

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing an alternative visual indicator such as a box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons have focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Users navigating by keyboard cannot see when the logo link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. This mobile menu button becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Dropdown menu items lose focus visibility for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing an alternative visual indicator. The search submit button lacks focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Account links in the header are not visible when focused by keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The "Learn more" links within feature cards are not visible when focused.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. Filter buttons lose focus visibility for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing an alternative visual indicator. Pricing toggle buttons are not visible when focused.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without replacement. The scrollable table region loses focus visibility for keyboard users who tab to it.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. FAQ accordion buttons are not visible when focused.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to activate when small.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum target size. Checkboxes are often difficult to hit precisely, especially on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible overflow, which makes the content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it. It fails to clip or size the element to 1px, so screen-reader-only text is displayed on screen, violating the intended purpose of the class.

issuesFound.title: Button motion animation lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties and has an :active state with translateY(1px) transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.

issuesFound.title: Skip link motion lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has a transform transition that moves the link vertically. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from this motion, and there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable it.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This makes text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities who rely on clear word boundaries, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.


issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional visual differentiation such as border-bottom or font-weight changes on hover/focus, links are only distinguishable by colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links rely on colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector and its :hover state only change colour to indicate interactivity. With text-decoration: none inherited from the global rule, these links rely solely on colour to distinguish them from static text.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on colour alone

issuesFound.title: Social links rely on colour alone
