issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes by toggling CSS classes, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never updated. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving the attribute missing until the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap and return focus
issuesFound.explanation: The code declares _trap but never instantiates createFocusTrap. When the drawer opens, focus is not trapped inside the navigation, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger and panel are registered, the code sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup but never initialises aria-expanded="false", leaving screen readers unaware that the panel is collapsible.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the dropdown panel does not update the trigger’s aria-expanded attribute, so assistive technologies cannot announce the state change.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving the attribute stale (likely false).

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared or validated successfully, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not clear aria-invalid, potentially leaving stale invalid states.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly, annual, etc.) act as toggle buttons but only update CSS classes; they do not set aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot indicate which period is active.

issuesFound.title: pricing custom switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The custom billing toggle (role="switch") updates the billing period but never updates aria-checked to reflect the on/off state, violating the Switch pattern requirements.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the empty state may appear, but no aria-live announcement informs screen reader users how many results are showing or that the view has changed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content without announcing the change to screen readers, even though STRINGS.faqOpened and STRINGS.faqClosed are defined for this purpose.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button moves focus to the skip link but does not announce the action, leaving screen reader users unaware that the page has scrolled to the top.

fpTitles: scrollToTop button missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function opens the dropdown panel and sets inst.open = true, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1368: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); inside the _openInstance function after inst.open = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function closes the dropdown panel and sets inst.open = false, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element to "false". Screen reader users will not be informed that the panel is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); inside the _closeInstance function after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = true, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2310: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside the open function after item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close function collapses the FAQ answer panel and sets item.open = false, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the answer is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2332: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); inside the close function after item.open = false;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function displays the mobile navigation drawer and sets _open = true, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); inside the open function after _open = true;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the mobile navigation drawer and sets _open = false, but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1342: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); inside the close function after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function manages filter tab activation by toggling CSS classes, but does not update the aria-selected attribute on the tab buttons. Since these implement a tab pattern (with roving tabindex), screen reader users need aria-selected to know which tab is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: Inside the forEach loop, add setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive)); after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen reader users will not be informed that the field contains an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the markInvalid function after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field remains marked as invalid for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid'); inside the markValid function after updating aria-describedby;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The register function initializes the dropdown with the panel hidden but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the panel is collapsed on initial page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); in the register function;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The init function finds the mobile navigation trigger button but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen reader users will not know the navigation is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after _initialised = true; in the init function (or after retrieving _trigger);

issuesFound.title: productFilter _updateCountBadge not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the product count text content when filters are applied, but this change is not announced to screen reader users because the element is not a live region. Users will not know the result count has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Add liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`); after _countBadge.textContent = String(count); in the _updateCountBadge function;

issuesFound.title: pricing _updateSavingsMessage not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateSavingsMessage function updates the savings message text when the billing period changes, but this dynamic content change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1990: Add liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent); after _savingsMsg.removeAttribute('hidden'); in the _updateSavingsMessage function;

issuesFound.title: productFilter _updateEmptyState not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateEmptyState function shows or hides the empty state message when no products match filters, but this visibility change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1422: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.noProductsFound || 'No products found matching your filters'); inside the if (count === 0) block after _emptyState.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open function reveals the mobile navigation drawer but does not activate a focus trap to keep keyboard focus within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer and access background content while the drawer is open, violating the modal dialog pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: Add if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); } _trap.activate(); inside the open function after _open = true;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing focus trap creation
issuesFound.explanation: The init function declares a _trap variable but never creates the focus trap instance for the mobile navigation drawer. This prevents proper focus management when the drawer opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); after _drawer is assigned in the init function;

fpTitles: mobileNav open missing aria-expanded update
dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
productFilter _updateEmptyState not announced via live region
mobileNav init missing focus trap creation

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not update its aria-expanded attribute when the menu opens or closes, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1224: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment.
  Line 1240: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after the `_open = false;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an initial aria-expanded attribute, so screen readers do not announce it as a collapsible control on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: After the line `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the overlay to background page elements.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, keyboard focus is not returned to the trigger button, leaving focus at an unpredictable location in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: Before the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`, insert `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria-expanded when their panels open or close, so screen readers cannot convey the visibility state of the submenu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1380: In `_openInstance`, insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.
  Line 1393: In `_closeInstance`, insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Newly registered dropdown triggers do not have an initial aria-expanded attribute set to false, violating the requirement that expandable controls indicate their initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: In the `register` function, after the line `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers do not toggle aria-expanded when items are expanded or collapsed, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current panel state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2100: In the `open` function, after `item.open = true;`, insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.
  Line 2120: In the `close` function, after `item.open = false;`, insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch open/close missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acting as a combobox does not update aria-expanded when the suggestions list opens or closes, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1850: In `_open`, after `_searchState.open = true;`, insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.
  Line 1865: In `_close`, after `_searchState.open = false;`, insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input lacks an initial aria-expanded attribute, preventing assistive technologies from recognizing it as an expandable control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1920: In `init`, after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, insert `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed to indicate which billing cycle is currently active, so screen readers cannot convey the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2300: In `_syncToggleState`, inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons do not indicate their active/pressed state via aria-pressed, making it impossible for screen reader users to know which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: In `_syncTabAriaStates`, inside the `forEach` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: In `markInvalid`, immediately after the null checks, insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, aria-invalid is not cleared, causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 810: In `markValid`, immediately after the null check for `field`, insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is only updated visually; screen reader users are not informed of how many results match the current filter criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In `applyFilters`, after the line `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, insert `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} product\${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price change is visible but not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: In `setPeriod`, after the line `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, insert `liveRegion.announce('Prices switched to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label + ' billing');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close missing focus return implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function accepts a `returnFocus` parameter but does not implement logic to return focus to the trigger button when closing the dropdown.

fpTitles: mobileNav close missing focus return
pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles submenu sections in the mobile navigation has aria-controls but does not indicate its state with aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the section is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 839: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button controls a panel via aria-controls but does not indicate its expanded/collapsed state with aria-expanded, failing to communicate state changes to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1513: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and provides no aria-label prop to the IconButton component, resulting in an empty accessible name that leaves screen reader users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button uses IconButton but provides no aria-label, resulting in an empty accessible name. The button contains only the "‹" character, which is not sufficiently descriptive for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button uses IconButton without an aria-label, leaving it without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1495: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton props.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not propagate aria-invalid to the underlying input element, preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: Add aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but fails to set aria-invalid on the textarea element, leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: Add aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the select element, preventing assistive technology from announcing error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: Add aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} to the select element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div onClick without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate this control to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 820: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler that calls onClose when Enter or Space is pressed.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div onClick without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has onClick={onClose} to dismiss the dialog when clicking outside, but it lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard support, making it inaccessible to keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler that calls onClose when Enter or Space is pressed.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation links apply a visual 'nav-link--current' class when item.current is true, but do not programmatically indicate the current page using aria-current="page", which is required for screen reader users to identify their location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 766: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links visually indicate the current page with 'mobile-nav__link--current' class but lack aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 839: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it displays a spinner and disables itself but does not communicate the busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 485: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as toggle buttons showing the active filter state visually, but they do not use aria-pressed to communicate which filter is active to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) show the selected state visually but do not communicate it programmatically via aria-pressed, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1312: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the Monthly button and aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the Annual button.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders an article element containing pricing information, but the article lacks an accessible name. It should reference the plan name heading via aria-labelledby to identify the landmark region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1357: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article element.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section contains a visually hidden h2 heading with id="stats-heading", but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby, leaving the landmark region unnamed for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish this navigation landmark from others on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation nav element lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, failing to identify the purpose of this navigation region to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 766: Add aria-label="Main navigation" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element without an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 839: Add aria-label="Mobile navigation" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section element lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, leaving this landmark region unnamed in screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 967: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: StatItem value not accessible to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component displays statistical values like "40,000+" which may not be announced correctly by screen readers. The Stat interface includes valueLabel for screen reader text (e.g., "40,000 plus"), but this property is not used in the component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1010: Add aria-label={stat.valueLabel} to the p element containing the value.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown chevron icon (▲/▼) is decorative but is not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the arrow character which may be confusing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the chevron.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator (asterisk) in FormGroup is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" separately from the "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the asterisk.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents assistive technology from knowing whether the submenu is currently visible.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls pointing to the panel but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the panel is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button in FaqItemComponent.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute to indicate whether the mobile navigation panel is currently open.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but ThemeToggle renders it without one, resulting in an icon-only button with no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton in ThemeToggle.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button is an IconButton rendered without the required aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without context for the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the previous IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button is an IconButton rendered without the required aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without context for the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the next IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute, so screen readers cannot convey the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element in TextInput.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing assistive technology from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the textarea element in Textarea.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not provided the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM, resulting in a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to FormGroup in NewsletterForm.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby when invalid, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the referenced element does not exist.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" and errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} to the first FormGroup in ContactForm.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby when invalid, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the referenced element does not exist.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup for email in ContactForm.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references 'contact-subject-error' in aria-describedby when invalid, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the referenced element does not exist.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" and errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup for subject in ContactForm.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby when invalid, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the referenced element does not exist.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" and errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup for message in ContactForm.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button shows selection state visually but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the monthly button in BillingToggle.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button shows selection state visually but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the annual button in BillingToggle.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is styled differently but lacks aria-current="page", so screen readers cannot identify which page is current in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the link in DesktopNav.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation is styled differently but lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the link in MobileNav.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks nav element lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks when multiple nav elements exist on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav in SkipLinks.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav nav element has an id but no aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Main" to the nav in DesktopNav.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section has a visually hidden h2 with id="stats-heading" but the section lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as the section's accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section in StatsBar.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section lacks aria-labelledby to expose the heading as the landmark's accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section in HeroSection.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which is necessary to signal to assistive technology that the content beneath the dialog is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div in MobileNav.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly placed aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div. This fails to properly mark the dialog as modal and may not correctly hide underlying content from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Remove aria-modal from overlay and add to dialog div in Modal.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component sets disabled and aria-disabled during loading but does not set aria-busy, which would communicate to assistive technology that the button is processing an action.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button in Button component.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true" to the required marker span in FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: StarRating star characters missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters inside the StarRating component are not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant announcements alongside the component's aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true" to the star character spans in StarRating.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus indicator. Outline-offset alone cannot produce a visible ring—it only positions where an outline would appear if one existed. Keyboard-only users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 242-245: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) rule for :focus-visible removes outline without providing a visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252-255: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto—the opposite of what a screen-reader-only utility should do. Content meant to be hidden from sighted users but announced by assistive technology is fully visible, defeating the purpose of the class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 262-271: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has min-height: 20px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments or touchscreens may struggle to activate the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 495-499: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon has min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px which are below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Icon-only buttons are difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 505-510: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-3); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px and height: 20px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to check boxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1119: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: .nav-toggle has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. With padding of 6px (0.5rem at 12px root), the total touch target is approximately 40px, below the 44px recommended minimum for comfortable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 522-530: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button have min-height: 28px. With vertical padding of 6px, the total touch target is approximately 40px, below the 44px recommended minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540-552: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on filter buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .filter-tabs button has min-height: 28px. With vertical padding of 6px, the total touch target is approximately 40px, below the 44px recommended minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 828-832: .filter-tabs button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background: none; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .pricing-toggle button has min-height: 28px. With vertical padding of 6px, the total touch target is approximately 40px, below the 44px recommended minimum.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925-929: .pricing-toggle button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: html { font-size: 12px; } sets the root font size to 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px instead of the recommended 16px. This causes all rem-based text to render smaller than intended, reducing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 81: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: body { word-spacing: -0.2em; } applies negative word-spacing globally, reducing the space between words. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision by making word boundaries harder to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 87: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; from the body rule, or change to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: body { letter-spacing: -0.05em; } applies negative letter-spacing globally, reducing the space between characters. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision by making letter recognition more difficult.
issuesFound.fix: Line 88: Remove letter-spacing: -0.05em; from the body rule, or change to letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Line-height below minimum on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: p { line-height: 1; } sets line-height to 1, which is below the recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text. This reduces readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision by making lines of text too close together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 117: p { line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual distinction
issuesFound.explanation: a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally without providing an alternative visual differentiator (such as increased font-weight, border-bottom, or background highlight). Links are distinguished from surrounding text by color alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 107: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add a visible non-color indicator such as border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; for links in body text.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty despite a comment claiming it suppresses "all transitions and animations." Users who prefer reduced motion still experience all animations because the override does nothing. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335-336: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Button active state transform without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } applies a spatial transform on user interaction, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion still experience the button movement, failing WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 458: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size set below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, establishing a root size below the recommended 16px minimum. All rem units throughout the stylesheet inherit from this undersized base, making body text and all proportional sizes smaller than intended for users without custom browser settings.
issuesFound.fix: Line 88: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class exposes content instead of hiding it
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — the opposite of a proper screen-reader-only implementation. Content meant to be visually hidden but accessible to assistive technology will be fully visible, defeating the purpose of the utility and breaking skip-link and other accessibility patterns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 359-368: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode removes focus outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible rule removes outline without providing any visible alternative. High Contrast Mode users cannot see keyboard focus, blocking navigation entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 344-346: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words and can cause words to appear merged, particularly harmful for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 96: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; from body selector.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which tightens character spacing across all text. This reduces readability for users with low vision or reading disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 96: Remove letter-spacing: -0.05em; from body selector.

issuesFound.title: .btn-sm touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 502: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: .btn-icon touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 pixels. Checkboxes are difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no protection
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, failing to disable any transitions or animations for users who prefer reduced motion. This violates the intent of WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383-384: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) and transition: transform var(--transition-slower), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty and does not disable the motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831-834: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Skip-link transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link has transition: transform var(--transition-base) but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 368: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, creating motion on interaction, but no reduced-motion override exists to disable this for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 456: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition and transform: translateY(-1px) on hover, but no reduced-motion override disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 755: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removal relies on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: The a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links globally. Without underlines, links must be distinguished by color alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 118: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add distinct non-color indicators (border-bottom, font-weight) for links in body text.

issuesFound.title: .btn-primary has insufficient color contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary uses background-color: #5588bb with color: #ffffff, yielding approximately 3.75:1 contrast ratio. At 12px base font-size, this fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum (4.5:1 for normal text).
issuesFound.fix: Line 461: .btn-primary { background-color: #4477aa; border-color: #4477aa; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); } (or use a darker shade achieving 4.5:1)

issuesFound.title: .btn-danger has severely insufficient color contrast

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces spacing between words and letters across all body text. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 107: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — color alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links globally without providing an alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or background highlight) for links in body text. This violates SC 1.4.1 Use of Color because links are distinguished by color alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 124: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } and add: .prose a, .content a { text-decoration: underline; } or provide border-bottom as alternative for non-prose links.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion block provides no override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and provides no overrides for animations or transitions defined elsewhere. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience all motion effects.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334-336: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition (transition: transform var(--transition-slower)), but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 724-727 is empty and does not disable the transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724-727: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label rule sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) which is white at 30% opacity. When rendered on the gradient background (--colour-brand-primary: #5588bb to --colour-brand-secondary: #bb88ee), the resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 884-887: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button during initialization. The initial state of the navigation (closed) is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2468: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not programmatically moved into it. Keyboard users must Tab through all preceding page content to reach the navigation, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and the ARIA APG dialog pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2373: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function sets aria-haspopup but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons. Dropdowns start hidden but their collapsed state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2649: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never sets aria-invalid="false" or removes the attribute. Fields may retain an invalid state announcement even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1860: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.goTo() missing live region announcement for slide change
issuesFound.explanation: The goTo() function updates slide visibility and aria-hidden attributes but the liveRegion.announce() call is present. However, the announcement message uses a template that may not clearly convey the slide content to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3960: `liveRegion.announce(`Slide ${target + 1} of ${_slides.length}: ${_slides[target].label}`);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The validation result renderer calls srUtils.markInvalid/markValid but those functions do not set aria-invalid. The field's invalid state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4297: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and Line 4301: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner focus trap not properly managed
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner creates a focus trap but the escape key handler is set to `escapeDeactivates: false`, preventing keyboard users from dismissing the banner with Escape. This creates a potential keyboard trap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5315: `const trap = createFocusTrap(_bannerEl, { escapeDeactivates: true });`

fpTitles: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the drawer

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch lacks aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned role="switch" but never receives aria-checked. Switch widgets require aria-checked to communicate their state to assistive technologies. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch never updates aria-checked on state change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle is clicked, aria-checked is never updated. For a role="switch", the aria-checked attribute must reflect the current on/off state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on the field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) and 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on the field
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, markValid removes the aria-describedby reference but does not remove aria-invalid. The field remains marked as invalid for screen readers even after the error is cleared.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button does not initialize aria-pressed when auto-play is disabled
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button only receives aria-pressed when play() is called. If the carousel starts in a paused state (due to prefers-reduced-motion or configuration), aria-pressed is never set, leaving screen readers unaware of the button's state.

issuesFound.title: Breadcrumb helper does not mark the last item as current page
issuesFound.explanation: The _processNav function has a comment indicating it should mark the last breadcrumb as the current page, but the actual implementation is missing. The code jumps from the comment directly to hiding separators. Screen readers cannot identify which breadcrumb represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply or change product filters, the visible count badge updates but screen readers receive no announcement. Users cannot perceive that the filter was applied or how many results remain. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually but screen readers receive no announcement. Users cannot perceive the billing period change. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not create or activate focus trap when drawer opens
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares `_trap = null` but never calls `createFocusTrap()` to create a focus trap, and never activates it when the drawer opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the navigation drawer into the page content behind it, violating WCAG 2.1.2 (No Keyboard Trap) in reverse — focus is not contained.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3207: add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true }); _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not return focus to trigger when drawer closes
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3228: add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button. Screen readers have no way to know the drawer's initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3265: add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on trigger elements
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state on trigger buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3445: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open = true and manipulates classes/attributes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4250: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not initialize aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init() function does not set initial aria-pressed state on billing period toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4675: add `_syncToggleState(initPeriod);` after `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);` (this will set aria-pressed via the updated _syncToggleState)

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not initialize aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.init() function does not set initial aria-pressed state on filter tab buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: add `_syncTabAriaStates();` after `_initialised = true;` (this will set aria-pressed via the updated _syncTabAriaStates)

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the field to an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field has failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never clears aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2495: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the aria-describedby cleanup block

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible card count changes but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3915: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');` inside the filterAnimation.run callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (monthly/annual), the visible prices update but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4610: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` before `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element when invalid. Screen readers cannot programmatically detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the textarea element when invalid. Screen readers cannot programmatically detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the select element when invalid. Screen readers cannot programmatically detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Required field asterisk marker announces bare asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk span marking required fields does not have aria-hidden, so screen readers will announce "asterisk" instead of relying on the visually hidden "(required)" text that follows.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page uses a CSS class to indicate current state but does not expose this programmatically. Screen reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page uses a CSS class to indicate current state but does not expose this programmatically. Screen reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on overlay instead of dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is on the overlay div but should be on the element with role="dialog". This prevents screen readers from correctly understanding the modal's boundaries.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires aria-label for icon-only buttons, but ThemeToggle only passes onClick and className. Screen readers will announce nothing or a generic "button" for this theme toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 740: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for navigating to the previous testimonial passes aria-controls but lacks the required aria-label prop. Screen readers cannot identify this button's purpose.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but role="dialog" is on the inner modal-dialog div. According to ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the same element as role="dialog". This causes incorrect modal behavior exposure to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1655: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line 1658: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: Hero video toggle button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Non-visible carousel slides not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The TestimonialsCarousel renders all slides in the DOM but only visually hides non-current slides. Without aria-hidden="true" on hidden slides, screen reader users may navigate to content that is not visually perceivable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1279-1291: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Informative badge incorrectly hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders the "Most popular" badge with aria-hidden="true", hiding meaningful information from screen reader users. The badge conveys important plan distinction information that should be accessible.

issuesFound.title: Complex statistic value not announced accessibly
issuesFound.explanation: The StatItem component displays complex formatted values like "40,000+" and "99.9%" without providing a screen-reader-friendly alternative. The valueLabel property exists but is not used, so AT may announce values unclearly (e.g., "forty zero zero zero plus").

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple navigation landmarks exist on a page, each should have a unique accessible name for screen reader users to distinguish them.

issuesFound.title: Decorative spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component renders a visual spinner (btn-spinner) when loading, but does not hide it from screen readers. While a VisuallyHidden loading label is provided, the decorative spinner element should also have aria-hidden="true" to prevent redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 454-457: {isLoading && (<><span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" /><VisuallyHidden>{loadingLabel}</VisuallyHidden></>)}
